source_jobs.title: Frontend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Capsule is building the world’s most intuitive AI-powered video editing tool, designed for modern teams. We’re a Series A startup led by two serial founders who have built and scaled creative platforms used by hundreds of millions of people. We’re backed by top-tier investors behind Webflow, Figma, Slack, and Replit.

We’ve grown quickly. In less than a year, 100+ enterprise teams—including HubSpot, ServiceNow, and Instacart—use Capsule to create high-quality, on-brand video content at scale.

We're hiring a Senior Frontend Engineer to help us build the next generation of web-based video editing. You’ll be a key contributor to our engineering culture and product direction, working closely with design and product in a zero-ego, high-ownership environment.






WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build beautiful, performant, responsive web UIs using React and Next.js

 - Translate designs into high-quality code—adding the nuance that turns "good" into "great"

 - Collaborate with design and product to shape new features from concept to launch
   Implement and maintain our design system and component library

 - Own integrations with internal and 3rd-party APIs

 - Identify and eliminate technical debt, simplify architecture, and improve performance

 - Champion quality through testing, code reviews, documentation, and best practices

 - Explore new technologies through rapid prototyping and R&D

 - Contribute to a high-velocity, collaborative engineering culture

 - Stay up-to-date with AI coding tools to gain leverage and velocity
   






WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years of professional frontend experience (React, Next.js, or similar frameworks)

 - A strong sense of product and design—your work should feel as good as it looks
   Experience building production apps from scratch

 - Solid knowledge of modern frontend architectures and web video technologies

 - Proficiency with Figma and collaborating closely with design

 - Strong communicator with high emotional intelligence

 - Entrepreneurial mindset with a bias toward action

 - Bonus points for:
   
   
   - API development with Node.js http://node.js
   
   - WebAssembly and/or WebGL
   
   - AI Engineering
   
   - Familiarity with CI/CD and APM tools






PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Salary between 100k-170k USD +equity

 - Comprehensive health coverage (medical, dental, vision)

 - Generous PTO

 - A diverse, mission-driven team with big ambitions and low ego

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Capsule is building the world’s most intuitive AI-powered video editing tool, designed for modern teams. We’re a Series A startup led by two serial founders who have built and scaled creative platforms used by hundreds of millions of people. We’re backed by top-tier investors behind Webflow, Figma, Slack, and Replit.

We’ve grown quickly. In less than a year since leaving public beta, 100+ enterprise teams—including HubSpot, ServiceNow, and Instacart—use Capsule to create high-quality, on-brand video content at scale.

We are seeking a skilled AI Engineer to join our team and help develop the next generation of our video editing tool. In this role, you will work closely with our founders and product and engineering teams to design, implement, and deploy ML models that power new and improved features for our product.

‍


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Research and evaluate new ML techniques and technologies that can be applied to our product

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify and prioritize ML feature development opportunities

 - Develop and implement ML models and algorithms to power these features

 - Optimize model performance and accuracy through testing and iterative development

 - Participate in code reviews and provide technical guidance to other team members

 - Staying up-to-date with the latest trends and best practices in ML engineering

 - Work fast and enjoy the rapid pace of an early stage startup

 - Have fun working with other highly-skilled team players in a zero-ego work environment

 - Bring new ideas to the table that expand the capabilities of the product and delight customers

Requirements

 - 3+ years of experience as an AI/ML engineer in a commercial software development setting

 - Computer Science or related degree

 - Strong proficiency in Python and at least one deep learning framework (e.g. PyTorch)

 - Experience with data preprocessing, feature engineering, and model selection and optimization

 - Experience building and deploying ML models in production environments

 - Strong problem-solving and communication skills

 - Highly-motivated, entrepreneurial-minded, high EQ

 - Strong communication and organization skills

 - Interest in video technology in general

 - Experience with CI/CD a plus

Benefits

 - Competitive compensation including salary and stock options

 - Comprehensive insurance benefits — medical, dental, vision, life

 - Generous paid time off

source_jobs.title: Lead Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
HI! 👋

Capsule is building the future of AI-powered video creation for enterprise teams. Led by serial founders who have scaled creative platforms to 100s of millions of users, we’re backed by top investors, including those behind Webflow, Figma, Slack, and Replit.



We’re growing fast. Just a few months after leaving public beta, Capsule is already helping leading brands like HubSpot, ServiceNow, and Instacart create professional, on-brand videos at scale.



We’re looking for an immensely talented and visionary product designer to join our founding team at Capsule. This is a unique opportunity for a highly-experienced product designer with a strong aesthetic/visual sensibility who is ready to make their mark and help us build the creative video tools of the future.



You’ll work directly with our co-founder and CEO and will have the opportunity to make an enormous impact on our company and product.




YOU

 - You’re a visionary designer who can articulate ideas with detail and precision

 - You love working through big ideas but obsess over the smallest details (subpixels)

 - You work fast and enjoy the rapid pace of an early-stage start up

 - You’re a swiss army knife of design. UX, UI, motion, visual – you can and want to do it all

 - You’re a Figma savant and know how to prototype and test designs with others

 - You love thinking in motion and have at least an interest in video/animation

 - You have fun working with other highly-skilled team players in a zero-ego work environment

 - You’re highly motivated, entrepreneurially minded and have off-the-charts EQ




THE JOB

 - Lead and execute all design projects (80% product, 20% brand/marketing/sales)

 - Work closely with co-founders to find elegant but practical solutions to design challenges

 - Bring ideas to the table to expand the capabilities of the product and delight customers

 - Rapidly produce multiple concepts and prototypes

 - Talk to customers to gather feedback to develop the best execution of every new feature

 - Build a world-class design team (3-5 hires planned for this year)




THE PERKS

 - Shape a video/design tool going after a $50B opportunity

 - Work with highly-motivated, talented, fun and creative people

 - Competitive compensation including salary and stock options

 - Comprehensive insurance benefits – medical, dental, vision

 - Generous paid time off

 - Hybrid work environment in LA—relocation covered

source_jobs.title: Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Strategic Account Executive to lead new business growth at Capsule. You’ll own the full sales cycle with enterprise prospects—from discovery to close—while building the relationships and business cases that bring Capsule into the world’s leading companies. While expansion within existing accounts will remain part of your remit, your primary focus will be on winning new logos and accelerating Capsule’s trajectory.
This is a high-impact, customer-facing role where you’ll shape how the market experiences Capsule and directly influence the company’s growth story.What You’ll Do

 - Build and close net-new enterprise deals by clearly articulating Capsule’s value and differentiation

 - Lead compelling demos and discovery conversations that connect prospect needs to Capsule’s use cases

 - Create urgency and momentum in deals by driving clear next steps, proposals, and executive alignment

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with SDRs, Marketing, and Product to ensure high-quality pipeline and messaging

 - Partner with Customer Success to ensure smooth handoffs and early expansion opportunities

 - Deliver accurate forecasts and consistently exceed targets through disciplined pipeline management

Outcomes we’re looking for:

 - Bring in new enterprise logos that become long-term Capsule customers

 - Consistently exceed quota while maintaining clean, predictable forecasts

 - Grow current customer accounts into larger enterprise partnerships by identifying and driving expansion opportunities

 - Build multi-threaded relationships that turn first deals into multi-team adoption

 - Position Capsule as a trusted partner by bringing market insights back to the team

You Might Be a Fit If You Have:

 - 5+ years of SaaS sales experience, with a focus on enterprise accounts

 - A track record of exceeding quota and driving growth in complex sales cycles

 - Excellent communication and relationship-building skills

 - A proactive, ownership-driven mindset - you see opportunities and take initiative

 - Bonus: experience selling to marketing, creative, or GTM teams

Compensation + Benefits

 - Salary range: $120-160K/$240K-320K OTE + equity

 - Comprehensive health benefits (medical, dental, vision, life)

 - Generous PTO

 - Remote-first culture with regular team offsites

 - Fast-growing team with meaningful opportunities for advancement

Why Join Capsule
We’re a tight-knit, zero-ego team that moves fast and loves what we do. At Capsule, you’ll have the chance to:

 - Shape the future of video tools for work

 - Work with world-class engineers, designers, and creatives

 - Join a company that believes fun is a competitive advantage

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
We’re Capsule—a venture-backed startup building AI-powered video tools for enterprise teams. Led by two serial founders who have scaled creative platforms for hundreds of millions of users, we’re backed by top investors, including backers of Webflow, Figma, Slack, Flexport, OnDeck, Masterclass, and Replit.



Capsule is the first video editing tool designed exclusively for work, and we’re growing fast. Since leaving public beta, our platform has already helped hundreds of enterprises—including HubSpot, ServiceNow, and Instacart—create professional, on-brand videos at scale.



The Role:

We’re looking for an Enterprise Sales Development Rep to help expand our existing outbound efforts into key target accounts. We’ve recently established our outbound motion and are looking for a driven, reflective and eager SDR to identify, prospect and contribute meaningful qualified pipeline opportunities for our Account Executive team to close. 



You’ll work closely with Sales, Marketing and Customer Success to become an expert on our ICP and TAM and drive meaningful conversations with prospects. If you thrive in a fast-moving startup environment and are excited to drive awareness of Capsule, we’d love to hear from you!



What You’ll Do:

 - Prospect into target accounts with personalized, thoughtful and scalable messaging across multiple channels. 



 - Complete thorough account research and account org chart mapping. 



 - Qualify inbound and outbound leads by assessing customer needs with our established qualification criteria. 



 - Handoff qualified opportunities to the sales team, scheduling meetings and follow-ups. 



 - Assist in Marketing efforts for event outreach, tradeshows and company sponsored events. 



 - Maintain impeccable CRM hygiene- logging and completing all necessary tasks to ensure data hygiene.

What We’re Looking For

1+ years of experience in Sales, Sales Development or a related role at a SaaS company.



 - Proven ability to build greenfield pipeline and meet/exceed goals.



 - A reflective, eager, driven and team-player collaborative attitude. 



 - Consultative approach to identifying pain and presenting Capsule Value Propositions in order to land initial meetings.



 - Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities.



 - Comfortable operating in a fast-moving, early-stage startup.



 - Passion for AI and video technology is a plus!



Why Join Us?

 - Competitive compensation including salary and equity.



 - Comprehensive benefits—medical, dental, vision, and life insurance.



 - Generous paid time off to recharge and stay inspired.



 - Work with a world-class team in a fast-growing, mission-driven company.



If you’re excited about creating an exceptional customer experience and shaping the future of AI-powered video editing, we’d love to hear from you!

source_jobs.title: Head of Demand Generation
source_jobs.description:
Hi! 👋

I'm Natalie Taylor http://linkedin.com/in/nataliecstaylor, Head of Marketing. And we're Capsule—a Series A https://www.linkedin.com/posts/champbennett_very-excited-to-announce-capsules-12m-series-activity-7318279051108835329-xRe8?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAABCQcEoBojkScYQ4o5kaqD2YkCbXjcEmPMk company building AI-powered video tools for enterprise teams. We're led by two serial founders who have built and scaled creative platforms for 100s of millions of people. Our investors include Innovation Endeavors, HubSpot Ventures, Bloomberg Beta, Swift Ventures, Human Ventures, and the same investors as iconic creative tools like Figma, Webflow, Frame.io http://Frame.io, and Replit.

We're growing fast. We have real product-market fit and are closing enterprise customers like HubSpot, ServiceNow, Zendesk, VRBO, Instacart, Carta, and more. Now we need to build the marketing engine to match.

 
 


THE ROLE

Here's the opportunity: inbound leads close at the highest rate by a wide margin—and we're generating very few of them. Right now, the majority of our pipeline comes from outbound channels. We need this person to change that ratio.

This is an exciting time to come into this role because we're not starting from zero: We have compelling customer stories https://capsule.video/customers, a strong events program, product launches shipping constantly, a sales team that's closing huge logos, and ops support. What we don't have is someone whose job is to turn all of that into demand—to make sure the right people find us, know who we are, and show up ready to buy.

You'll own top-of-funnel demand creation: running campaigns that generate net new leads, getting more eyes on what we're already building, and working closely enough with sales and ops that your work connects to actual pipeline. You'll set the strategy and do the work. There's no one to hand things off to, and we're not looking for someone who needs direction on where to start.

The person we're looking for has done this before at a startup and understands what it takes to build inbound in a complex enterprise sales environment. We expect meaningful signal within 3 months: well-run experiments, early indicators of what's working, and the beginning of a repeatable inbound engine.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL OWN:

Your north star is inbound pipeline growth.

 - Integrated campaigns: Plan and run campaigns that connect our product launches, customer stories, and events into demand that reaches the right people. We have a lot of great raw material—your job is to get more eyes on it and turn it into leads.

 - Content distribution: Think constantly about where our audience is and how to reach them. We punch above our weight on content and assets. Your job is to build the distribution engine behind it, so our launches, customer stories, and event content actually reach the right people.

 - ABM: Build and run account-based programs against a defined target list. Work with sales to identify the right accounts, build the right message, and get in front of the right people.

 - Paid amplification: Own paid channels (primarily LinkedIn) as one lever in the broader mix. You don't need to be a paid media specialist, but you need to run these channels intelligently and know when to scale them up or shut them off. There's no defined media budget today; part of your job is building the case for where we should invest and how much.

 - Channel experimentation: We're not fully invested in any single inbound channel yet; part of your job is figuring out where the highest-leverage opportunities are—SEO/AEO, communities, partnerships, organic social—running real experiments, and doubling down on what works.

 
 


YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 5–8 years of B2B demand gen or campaigns experience; you’ve built demand gen or inbound from near-zero at a startup before and are ready to fully own it here

 - Have worked directly alongside a sales team in a complex, enterprise B2B

source_jobs.title: GTM Operations Analyst (Contractor)
source_jobs.description:
GTM Operations Analyst (Contract)

Capsule · Remote

Capsule is a venture-backed startup building AI-powered video tools for enterprise teams. We’re backed by top investors—including backers of Webflow, Figma, Slack, Flexport, OnDeck, Masterclass, and Replit—and our platform has already helped hundreds of enterprises, including HubSpot, ServiceNow, and Instacart, create on-brand video at scale.

We’re hiring a GTM Operations Analyst Contractor to help run the data and the systems underneath our go-to-market motion. This role is ⅓ marketing ops, ⅓ sales ops, ⅓ GTM engineering / intelligence — and 100% AI-forward. All three are equally important. The person in this seat is fluent across the whole stack, not specialized to one corner of it.




THE ROLE

Each leg of the role matters.

Sales ops keeps the pipeline honest — clean stages, accurate forecasts, reps spending time on the right accounts. Marketing ops turns demand into qualified pipeline — clean attribution, ICP-gated routing, lifecycle scoring, intent activation. GTM engineering / intelligence is the connective tissue — the agents, automations, and signals that make the whole motion compound instead of repeat. Most companies underinvest in all three until something breaks. We’re not waiting.

You’ll operate inside a stack that’s already AI-native. We build agents and skills with Claude Code, automate workflows that used to take 6-person ops teams, and expect every operator on the team to ship AI tooling — not just use it. If that sounds like the version of ops you’ve been waiting to do, we should talk.

This is a short-term contract role with the possibility of converting to full-time based on performance and company needs.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Contribute to running HubSpot — Marketing Hub + Sales Hub Enterprise. Properties, workflows, lifecycle, lead scoring, sequences, reporting, integrations.

 - Contribute to marketing ops: ICP gating at the form, lifecycle scoring, nurture and reactivation flows, MQL-to-SQL quality enforcement.

 - Stand up our intent data layer and wire signals into HubSpot so SDRs only work accounts with real buying activity.

 - Build, extend, and operate AI workflows with Claude Code. If you can describe an ops SOP, you can ship a skill that runs it.

 - Partner with the VP of Ops to support Demand Gen, the SDR function, Sales, and CS.
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years across marketing ops, sales ops, RevOps, or GTM engineering at a B2B SaaS company. Bonus if you’ve worn more than one of those hats.

 - HubSpot admin chops are non-negotiable. You’ve built lifecycle, scoring, attribution, and reporting from the ground up. You can debug a broken workflow at 9pm.

 - Hands-on with Claude Code or equivalent agentic AI tooling. You’ve actually shipped skills, agents, or automations — not just prompted a chatbot. You think about ops the way an engineer thinks about systems.

 - Amplitude (or Mixpanel/Heap) at a product-led company. You can instrument events and pipe signals into the GTM stack.

 - Comfortable across the modern GTM tool surface: Apollo, ZoomInfo, Gong, Outreach/Salesloft, Clay, Webflow forms, Slack, Notion, BigQuery / a warehouse.

 - SQL good enough to pull your own data; Python or JS good enough to write a script when no-code runs out.

 - Bias to action. Allergic to busy-work. You’d rather automate it than do it twice.

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
CUSTOMER SUCCESS MANAGER

Capsule is building the world’s most intuitive AI-powered video editing tool, designed for modern teams. We help brand-driven software companies scale video production across their entire org, without scaling their creative team headcount.

We’re a Series A startup led by two serial founders who have built and scaled creative platforms used by hundreds of millions of people. We’re backed by top-tier investors behind Webflow, Figma, Slack, and Replit.

Our customers are companies that take their brand seriously. Your job is to make sure they stay, grow, and never have a reason to look elsewhere.



 
 


THE ROLE

You'll own a high-touch book of mid-market and enterprise accounts with a clear mandate: onboard them, retain them, grow them, and make sure they're getting real value from Capsule every day.



This is a commercial and consultative CS role. You’ll have expansion and upsell targets and you're the kind of CSM who sees that as a good thing, because the best expansion conversations happen naturally when customers are genuinely successful.



You'll identify where accounts can go deeper with Capsule, partner with Sales to bring those opportunities to close, and run EBRs that make executives eager to expand their relationship with us as we drive value by continuously pushing the envelope on what is possible with video.



Since self-serve video creation is still fairly new, your role will include change management and strategic planning with stakeholders to help turn non-video creators into weekly video creators.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO



Cultivate Strong, Consultative Relationships

 - Own and facilitate the onboarding and training of new customer accounts with a focus on demonstrating value early and often.

 - Own net revenue retention across your customer base - monitor health signals, catch risk early, and intervene before a quiet account becomes a churn risk

 - Maintain active and consultative relationships across all accounts; we want to support immediate goals but also understand their (often shifting) longer term vision

 - Build multi-threaded relationships so you're never one champion departure away from a renewal problem

 - Conduct EBRs and strategic planning sessions in order to cultivate relationships and highlight value for VP/C-Suite level stakeholder



Grow the book

 - Carry an expansion and upsell quota; identify opportunities within your accounts and drive them to close in partnership with Sales

 - Map white space — teams, use cases, and workflows within your accounts that aren't yet using Capsule but should be

 - Frame expansion conversations around customer outcomes and organizational goals/priorities



Partner internally on renewals and risk

 - Forecast renewals accurately and flag risk early

 - Work closely with Sales on renewal strategy and expansion pipeline so handoffs are clean and nothing falls between the cracks

 - Bring customer intelligence back to Product and GTM, in order to inform road map, advocate for customer needs, and refine the ICP



Grow CS Team Impact

 - As a member of a small team, you’ll take on side projects that help us scale.

 - These projects will include things like training videos, workflow documentation, webinar production, engagement activations, AI workflow automations

 
 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5-7+ years in enterprise CS, Account Management, or post-sales SaaS

 - Demonstrated success driving user adoption and influencing new workflows across teams

 - Demonstrated track record of hitting NRR and/or expansion targets — you've owned a number and hit it

 - Strong experience partnering with sales on expansion planning and execution

 - Eager to get in the weeds and comfortable with building the plane as we fly at an ever increasing speed



Skills

 - Commercially sharp: you can identify expansion opportunities, co-build the business case, and carry them to close alongside Sales

 - Data-fluent: you use product usage signals,

notes: Capsule is a Series A startup building AI-powered video tools for enterprise teams.
